refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. It is solution-neutral as it specifies 'compatible with safe remote operation' without prescribing how to achieve it. The refinement clarifies the latency requirement as ensuring safety, maintaining problem domain focus.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency does not compromise safe remote operation.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency data transfer
Design a real-time data compression and decompression mechanism to reduce transmission time
Establish a prioritized data flow control to ensure critical control commands have minimal delay
Incorporate local predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication latency
Use an end-to-end latency monitoring and adaptive adjustment system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Establish a prioritized data flow control to ensure critical control commands have minimal delay

potential_sources: Interdependence between communication protocol and data compression mechanisms
Tight coupling between predictive control and latency monitoring systems
coupling_check.notes: Selecting prioritized data flow control as the DP maintains logical independence from specific communication protocols and predictive algorithms, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not impose additional latency constraints
Prioritization mechanisms can be implemented without introducing significant overhead
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including adaptive latency compensation mechanisms at the DP level to enhance robustness against variable network conditions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing network and data flow control within the system

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Data flow for monitoring system status and performance

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection for data transmission

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution manages network and data flow control; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Vehicle module is broader and includes many subsystems; data flow control is only a part, leading to moderate diffusion.
Teleoperation involves remote control but data flow control is shared with network management, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety monitoring; data flow control is peripheral, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of prioritized data flow control, as it directly manages network and data transmission aspects, ensuring minimal delay for critical commands.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if control commands are tightly integrated with remote operation logic.
Dependency on Network Link interface stability may affect containment effectiveness.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks persist, consider refining the DP to explicitly separate network-level data flow control from higher-level teleoperation command logic to improve modular clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of establishing prioritized data flow control logically addresses the FR of supporting teleoperation with minimal latency for safe remote operation. Prioritizing critical control commands aligns with the acceptance criterion of p95 latency ≤ 150 ms, ensuring safe operation.

diffusion_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation module if control commands are tightly integrated with remote operation logic.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and minimal diffusion (containment score 8, diffusion score 2), making it an appropriate boundary for the DP. Other modules show moderate to high diffusion, supporting this containment choice.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be grounded in measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) under agreed network conditions using telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which manages network and data flow control, aligning well with the FR's focus on teleoperation latency and safety.

recommended_revisions: If containment risks persist, refine the DP to explicitly separate network-level data flow control from higher-level teleoperation command logic to improve modular clarity and reduce diffusion risk.

decision.justification: All gates have passed, indicating that the current DP and IM allocation adequately address the FR with acceptable containment and verification feasibility. However, the high safety criticality and noted diffusion risk with the Teleoperation module suggest that further decomposition of the FR into more detailed sub-FRs could help clarify boundaries and reduce integration risks. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will enable more precise allocation and verification, improving modular clarity and safety assurance.
next_level_sub_frs: Ensure prioritized data flow control for critical commands
Maintain overall network latency within p95 ≤ 150 ms
Isolate control command processing from teleoperation logic to reduce diffusion risk
Provide telemetry and network link interface for latency verification

assumptions: The current DP logically satisfies the FR acceptance criteria.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate IM boundary for the DP.
Verification of latency is feasible with existing telemetry and network interfaces.
open_risks: Potential overlap and diffusion with Teleoperation module if control commands are tightly integrated.
Containment risks if DP is not sufficiently separated from higher-level teleoperation logic.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under agreed network conditions to confirm p95 ≤ 150 ms.
Analysis of module interfaces to confirm minimal diffusion and high cohesion.
